findings.title: Gate-id correlation guard ineffective when resume_run() called without gate_id parameter
findings.problem: The resume_run() function has an optional gate_id parameter (defaults to None). When called without this parameter, the gate-correlation guard in gates.py:140 becomes ineffective. The guard is designed to prevent a misrouted operator response from finalizing a gate other than the one parked. However, when gate_id is not passed to resume_run(), the resume_value dict never includes gate_id, so the correlation check 'resp.get("gate_id") not in (None, gate_id)' always passes (treating None as valid) and any response is accepted regardless of which gate should be receiving it. Tests call resume_run() without gate_id, confirming this is a reachable code path.
findings.best_practice_violated: Security feature incompletely enforced - gate-correlation validation documented as necessary (pass-4 #3) but optional in implementation. Docstring says 'Without it the guard is unreachable' and 'The ADR-0014 channel should record and pass back the gate_id it surfaced' but provides no mechanism for ADR-0014 to pass gate_id through resume_run().
findings.proposed_fix: Make gate_id a required parameter in resume_run() by removing the default value (change 'gate_id: str | None = None' to 'gate_id: str'). All call sites (currently only in tests) must explicitly pass the gate ID. Alternatively, implement auto-detection of which gate is currently parked and validate correlation automatically without requiring the caller to pass it.

result.title: Gate-id correlation guard ineffective when resume_run() called without gate_id parameter
result.verification: Verified in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/graph.py:185-216 and /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/nodes/gates.py:140. The resume_run() function has an optional gate_id parameter (defaults to None, line 186). When called without this parameter, line 213-214 condition fails (gate_id is None), so "gate_id" is NOT added to resume_value. The gate-correlation guard at gates.py:140 checks: `resp.get("gate_id") not in (None, gate_id)`. When gate_id is missing from the response, resp.get("gate_id") returns None. The check becomes `None not in (None, current_gate_id)` which is False, so the guard does NOT re-prompt and accepts the response unconditionally. This is confirmed as a known documented issue: the docstring at graph.py:198-200 explicitly states "Without it the guard is unreachable." Test evidence: test_resume_run_approves_gate_1 (line 128-137) and test_resume_run_recovers_from_malformed_decision (line 140-156) both call resume_run() without gate_id and pass, but only test gate_1. The correlation test test_gate_id_correlation_rejects_mismatched_decision (line 75-89) DOES pass gate_id and verifies the guard works when gate_id is provided. The vulnerability is reachable in a multi-gate scenario (gate_1 → gate_2) where someone calls resume_run() without gate_id on a run parked at gate_2—the guard would be ineffective. This is not in the WONTFIX list (pass 7 rationale in HARDENING_LOG line 288-302).
result.recommended_action: Make gate_id a required parameter in resume_run() by removing the default value (change line 186 from `gate_id: str | None = None` to `gate_id: str`). All call sites must explicitly pass the gate ID. Update the docstring to reflect this is mandatory. Add a test that calls resume_run() without gate_id to catch regression if the default is accidentally restored. Alternatively, implement auto-detection of which gate is currently parked and validate correlation automatically without requiring the caller to pass it—but this is more complex and the simple fix (required parameter) is preferred.

findings.title: Missing [tool.setuptools] configuration breaks package discovery in src-layout build
findings.problem: The pyproject.toml uses a src-layout (packages in src/) but lacks explicit [tool.setuptools] configuration. Modern setuptools auto-discovery from the root finds packages as 'src', 'src.agents', 'src.infrastructure', 'src.nodes' (with src. prefix), which is incorrect. The built wheel would install packages under site-packages/src/ instead of site-packages/agents/, site-packages/infrastructure/, etc. This breaks imports and makes the installed package unusable.
findings.best_practice_violated: PEP 517/621 build systems with src-layout require explicit package-dir configuration in [tool.setuptools] to ensure correct discovery and build output. Auto-discovery is implicit and fragile (vulnerable to setuptools version changes).
findings.proposed_fix:
Add explicit [tool.setuptools] configuration to pyproject.toml:

[tool.setuptools]
package-dir = { "" = "src" }
packages = ["agents", "infrastructure", "nodes"]

Alternatively, use dynamic discovery with proper exclusion:

[tool.setuptools]
package-dir = { "" = "src" }

[tool.setuptools.find]
where = ["src"]

And exclude tests from the distribution (currently included inadvertently).

result.title: Missing [tool.setuptools] configuration breaks package discovery in src-layout build
result.verification: Read /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/pyproject.toml (lines 1-56): No [tool.setuptools] section exists. Verified actual project structure: src/ contains __init__.py + subpackages (agents/, infrastructure/, nodes/), plus modules (checkpointer.py, graph.py, reasoning_client.py, state.py). Examined dist-info top_level.txt from current editable install (/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/hc_grc-0.1.0.dist-info/top_level.txt): Lists [__init__, agents, checkpointer, graph, infrastructure, nodes, reasoning_client, state], proving setuptools flattened src/ contents as top-level. Checked import patterns in src/agents/data_acquisition/agent.py: Uses relative imports like `from ...state import HCGRCState`, which depend on src/ being on sys.path. Verified .pth file (/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/__editable__.hc_grc-0.1.0.pth) contains `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src`, masking the defect in editable mode. Confirmed tests use `from src.agents` imports that only work because conftest.py (line 17) adds repo root to sys.path. The defect is HIDDEN in development but would cause ImportError in a normal wheel install due to broken relative import resolution.
result.recommended_action: Add explicit [tool.setuptools] configuration to pyproject.toml to fix src-layout discovery. The minimum fix is: [tool.setuptools] / package-dir = { "" = "src" } / [tool.setuptools.find] / where = ["src"]. This ensures setuptools correctly discovers agents/, infrastructure/, nodes/ as site-packages/agents/, site-packages/infrastructure/, site-packages/nodes/ (not site-packages/src/*). Also exclude tests from wheel by adding exclude = ["tests"] to [tool.setuptools.find] section. This addresses both the package-dir ambiguity AND prevents test code from shipping in distributions.
